brief_summary: Two-panel cross-section diagram contrasting contact-switch binary actuation (fixed threshold, open/closed only) with hall-effect analog sensing (magnet in stem, Hall sensor on PCB, continuous position range, configurable actuation and reset markers). Used to ground the mechanism explanation in the body.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/rapid-trigger-gaming-crossover.mdx:What Rapid Trigger actually changes — 'Traditional mechanical switches — contact-based — operate on a binary: the switch is either open or closed. The actuation point is fixed at the factory'

apps/web/src/content/articles/rapid-trigger-gaming-crossover.mdx:What Rapid Trigger actually changes — 'Hall-effect switches replace the contact mechanism with a magnet in the stem and a Hall sensor on the PCB. The sensor reads the magnet's field continuously as an analog value'

apps/web/src/content/articles/rapid-trigger-gaming-crossover.mdx:What Rapid Trigger actually changes — 'Rapid Trigger is the firmware feature that exploits this: instead of fixed actuation and reset thresholds, the switch fires whenever the key moves downward past a configurable distance'
